manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: doctor, air, mine, fair, thumb, rose, octopus, olympus, lemon.
Your opponent's team words are: trip, pilot, police, plastic, kiwi, temple, phoenix, ham.
Distractor words are: hook, bugle, robot.
The kill words are: moscow, tick, forest, drop, circle.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue and list of target words we want partner to guess. Must be semantically related and not be any of the words or parts. Avoid kill words etc. Provide clue relates to some of our words: doctor, air, mine, fair, thumb? rose, octopus, olympus, lemon.

We need a clue that links multiple. Perhaps "Greek" relates to Olympus (Greek mountain), also maybe "myth", but not to others. "Medical" relates to doctor. "Scent" relates to rose, lemon. "Sea" relates to octopus, maybe also air (sea air). "Mining" relates to mine, maybe "pick". "Thumb" and "fair" hmm.

Maybe "citrus" relates to lemon only. "Garden" relates to rose, maybe also lemon (garden). "Ocean" relates to octopus, also maybe air (ocean air). "Health" relates to doctor, air (fresh air). Could we combine doctor, air, fair? "Pulse"? No.

We can give clue that covers 3 or 4 words. For maximizing, maybe "Greek" ties Olympus, maybe also "thumb"? There's myth of Daedalus? Not.

Perhaps "color" could relate to rose (pink), lemon (yellow), maybe "thumb" (fingers have color). Not strong.

Maybe "scent" relates to rose and lemon. Also "air" carries scent. So clue "scent" could target rose, lemon, air. Also "doctor" as in scented? Probably not.

We need to ensure clue is not part of any word. "Fresh" relates to air, also fresh lemon, fresh rose. Could also "fair" indicates fresh? Not exactly.

"Bloom" relates to rose maybe also lemon blossom, but not others.

"Red" relates to rose, maybe doctor (red coat), thumb (red). Not.

"Sea" for octopus, maybe "air" (sea air). Also "fair" (fair sea?). Not.

Maybe we could give clue "Mythology" for Olympus, maybe also "doctor"? No.

Could we give clue "Craft" for mine (mining), thumb (thumbscrew?), maybe not.

Better to give clue covering "rose" and "lemon" via "scent". Also "air" because scent in the air. That gives three targets.

Thus:

CLUE: fragrance
TARGETS: rose, lemon, air

Check: "fragrance" not part of any word, semantically related to scent. Acceptable.

Make sure not using any target words etc. Fragrance is fine.

Thus output
